q: How accurate are these pet calorie calculators?
a: They're a solid starting point using formulas from APOP and veterinary research, but not a replacement for your vet. Every dog and cat is different.

q: Is the old 'multiply by 7' rule for dog years wrong?
a: Yeah, it's outdated. Modern research shows dogs age faster in their first two years then slow down. Our calculator uses the epigenetic clock formula adjusted for breed size.

q: Do I need to create an account to use these tools?
a: Nope. No account, no sign-up, no email. Every tool works directly in your browser.

q: How do I know if my pet is overweight?
a: Try our Pet BMI Calculator or do the rib test at home: can you feel the ribs with light pressure? If not, consult your vet.

q: How much water should my dog drink per day?
a: General rule: 1 ounce per pound of body weight. Our Water Intake Calculator gives a customized number based on diet and activity.
Some links are affiliate links but we only recommend products we'd use for our own pets. Read our Affiliate Disclosure for details.

q: Can I use these for puppies and kittens?
a: Our calorie tools work best for adults. Puppies and kittens have different needs — consult your vet.

q: What is a healthy BMI range for dogs?
a: Dogs use a Body Condition Score (1-9). The sweet spot is 4-5: ribs easily felt but not visible.

q: Why do indoor and outdoor cats have different calorie needs?
Do you offer personalized diet plans?
a: No. We're not veterinary nutritionists. Our tools give a starting point for vet conversations.
Reassess after spay/neuter, weight changes, activity shifts, or every 6 months alongside vet visits.

q: Is this site run by veterinarians?
a: No. I'm Morgan - a longtime pet owner. All calculators are based on published research, not professional medical advice.
